html:
Books are items craftable by Wickerbottom. They can be read several times at the cost of sanity to trigger several type of events. Only Wickerbottom and Maxwell can use the books, although Maxwell can only obtain them legitimately by swapping with Wickerbottom at the end of Adventure Mode.

Player can continue reading the books even at 0 sanity without paying any additional sanity cost. Players may use any of the standard ways to regain Sanity, consign themselves to fighting shadow creatures.

Contents

  • 1 Birds of the World
  • 2 Applied Horticulture
  • 3 Sleepytime Stories
  • 4 On Tentacles
  • 5 The End is Nigh!
  • 6 Joy of Volcanology
  • 7 Tips
  • 8 Trivia
  • 9 Gallery
  • 10 References

Birds of the World

Exclusive to:  

Birds of the World calls 20 to 30 Birds to land between 1.5 and 3 Tiles. It does not work during Hurricane Season in the Shipwrecked DLC. The type of spawned Birds depends on the season and turf the birds land on.

Birds of the World may need to be combined with Sleepytime Stories, ideally right before the night so the birds remain asleep for the duration of the night and not just duration of the book. The birds may also be frozen with Ice Flingomatics. There are also other sleepless way of killing, such as Winona's catapults.

This book is good for farming Krampus and Feathers when a proper farm is made on the Lunar Island.

Applied Horticulture

Exclusive to:  

Applied Horticulture grows crops and plants within 7.5 Tiles instantly, while Trees grow one stage every use. It does not work on unfertilized or withered crops or plants. Some plants, such as grass, do not grow in Winter.

Sleepytime Stories

Exclusive to:  

Sleepytime Stories causes Mobs within 7.5 Tiles to fall asleep about 2 seconds after reading. The duration of sleep depends on the target and the effect is broken when it is attacked.

Sleepytime Stories can be used instead of the Pan Flute, for example for shaving Beefalos during the day and during mating season, calming Dragonfly, stopping Bee Queen's attacks and beetles, stopping Depths Worm and Hound attacks, slowing down or completely stopping most boss and giants attacks or stealing guarded items from Set Pieces.

On Tentacles

Exclusive to:  

On Tentacles creates up to 3 Tentacles between 3m to 8m. Less tentacles are created when there are more obstructions in the spawning zone. Burnt structures, destroyed walls, trees, a boulder or another tentacle are examples of obstructions.

Tentacles from On Tentacles generally produces more Tentacle Spots than it consumes. They are also a source of Tentacle Spikes, a great melee weapon.

The book can be used to build defenses or traps, as the Tentacles are hostile to most Mobs in the game. Tentacle pits can be built by having a ring of tree stumps and safely reading On Tentacles 15-21 times in that ring.

The End is Nigh!

Exclusive to:  

The End is Nigh! generates 16 consecutive Lightning strikes between 0.75 and 3.75 tiles.

In the Reign of Giants (RoG) and Shipwrecked (SW) DLCs, any bolt of lightning from The End is Nigh! can strike Wickerbottom when used. The player should consider wearing lightning insulated clothing, such as the Eyebrella (RoG), Snakeskin Jacket/Hat (SW), or the Dumbrella (SW).

Joy of Volcanology

Exclusive to:

Joy of Volcanology creates 4 Dragoon Eggs falling at the player's location. It replaces "On Tentacles" in the Don't Starve: Shipwrecked DLC. Joy of Volcanology has the same functionality as the Volcano Staff, except it is less precise.

Fallen Dragoon Eggs sometimes create Lava Pools on the ground. When Lava pools burn out they leave behind Rocks , Ash and Charcoal. Lava Pools can be extinguished directly with Ice, which leaves behind a single Obsidian instead. This allows Wickerbottom to easily farm Rocks, Charcoal and Obsidian.

Tips

  • Wickerbottom's perk does not synergize with effect of The Everything Encyclopedia. Therefore, both her and other survivors will require at least Science Machine to prototype tier two magic items.

Trivia

  • The text "Ia! Ia!" in the description for The End is Nigh! refers to the phrase "Iä! Iä! Cthulhu fhtagn!", a chant from the Cthulhu Mythos of H. P. Lovecraft.
  • The sanity reduction from reading possibly could suggest that these books contain knowledge which humans were never meant to know, similar to the Necronomicon and Pnakotic Manuscripts from the writings of H.P. Lovecraft. Although Wurt can gain sanity by reading some of the books, this is likely because she reads them for personal enjoyment rather than to utilize possibly forbidden magic.
  • According to Wilson in Don't Starve Together, Sleepytime Stories is "just 500 pages of telegraph codes". This implies that the book bores those affected to sleep.
  • Sleepytime Stories and The End is Nigh! were originally named Bedtime Stories and Conflagration respectively.[1]
  • Previously, Don't Starve Together also featured the book "Applied Horticulture", but with the Reap What You Sow update, which redesigned the farming system, it was replaced by the book "Horticulture, Abridged".
  • When Wickerbottom inspects "Birds of the World", she mentions the Western Screech Owl. This is a reference to the mod the developers made titled The Screecher.
  • Several characters introduced with the Hamlet DLC have unimplemented quotes for Wickerbottom's books:
    • Wagstaff: Birds of the World ("I cannot access the knowledge within it. How frustrating!"), Applied Horticulture ("Much more than a collection of facts."), Sleepytime Stories ("Is this ink imbued with dark fuel?! Fascinating!"), On Tentacles ("What prevents me from using this?"), The End is Nigh! ("Oh, I do wish I could access its power.").
    • Wheeler: Birds of the World ("Will it help me get back in the air?"), Applied Horticulture ("I'm not digging this one."), Sleepytime Stories ("A sleeper hit."), On Tentacles ("I'm guessing it's not a children's book."), The End is Nigh! ("Feel good story of the year.").
    • Wormwood and Wilba do have quotes too.
  • Webber's comment on "Practical Rain Rituals" may be a reference to the superstition that killing a spider will make it rain. Less likely it references the Aztecs ritually sacrificing children to The Rain God, hoping for rain. Few other characters reference the use of a ritual.
  • All the books was Character-Specific Item, but its changed in Don't Starve Mega Update.
  • Since Applied Horticulture was previously present in Don't Starve Together, character quotes remain in the files. Most of them do not differ in any way from the original game, except for the presence of quotes from exclusive characters and a modified Warly quote

Gallery

  • Book as seen in the poster for the Strange New Powers update.
  • The End is Nigh! as seen on the 2017 Valentine Cards.
  • Books as seen on the 2018 Valentine Cards.
  • The End is Nigh:Gilded Edition as a part of Hot lava customization
  • Helpbook (The End is Nigh!) emoji from official Klei Discord server.
  • Unused Shakespeare Book
  • Unused Shakespeare Book on Water

References

  1. ↑ (Archive) Character Rebalance Discussion Thread. Posted on June 12, 2013.
Tools and WeaponsCraftable Items

Together
Books are items craftable by Wickerbottom. They can be read several times at the cost of sanity to trigger several type of events. Only Wickerbottom and Maxwell can use the books, although Maxwell can only obtain them legitimately by swapping with Wickerbottom at the end of Adventure Mode.
Player can continue reading the books even at 0 sanity without paying any additional sanity cost. Players may use any of the standard ways to regain Sanity, consign themselves to fighting shadow creatures.

Birds of the World[edit | edit source]
Birds of the World
"1000 species: habits, habitats, and calls."
Object Info
Perk
Call Birds.
-50 per use
3 uses
45 sec.
Can be Burned
Does not stack
Crafting
×2 ×2 ( only)
Tab
Tier
Always available
Miscellaneous
"book_birds"
Exclusive to:
“No point studying when I can just wing it.”
–Wilson
“Less reading, more burning!”
–Willow
“Book is for the birds!”
–Wolfgang
“Will it teach me to fly away from here?”
–Wendy
“UPLOADING AVIAN FACTOIDS TO DATABASE...”
–WX-78
“The expurgated version, but it has my favorite: Megascops kennicottii.”
–Wickerbottom
“I already know more than enough aboot those things.”
–Woodie
“What a wasteful misuse of magic.”
–Maxwell
“I cannot access the knowledge within it. How frustrating!”
–Wagstaff
“Tö rule the skies!”
–Wigfrid
“This one has pictures!”
–Webber
“I think I'll just look at real birds instead.”
–Walani
“I had hoped it was a poultry cookbook.”
–Warly
“This book means to bewitch the birds!”
–Woodlegs
“TIS NAUGHT BUT SCRIBBLERY!”
–Wilba
“Pretty pictures”
–Wormwood
“Will it help me get back in the air?”
–Wheeler
“I was never much of a book learner.”
–Winona
“Humans share their knowledge so freely.”
–Wortox
“Read this one, Wicker-lady!”
–Wurt
“I don't want to brag... but I do have a badge in birdwatching.”
–Walter
“Time and birds sure do fly.”
–Wanda

Birds of the World may need to be combined with Sleepytime Stories, ideally right before the night so the birds remain asleep for the duration of the night and not just duration of the book. The birds may also be frozen with Ice Flingomatics. There are also other sleepless way of killing, such as Winona's catapults.
This book is good for farming Krampus and Feathers when a proper farm is made on the Lunar Island.
Applied Horticulture[edit | edit source]
Applied Horticulture
"On the care and feeding of plants."
Object Info
Perk
Grow crops.
-33 per use
5 uses
45 sec.
Can be Burned
Does not stack
Crafting
×2 ×1 ×1 ( only)
Tab
Tier
Always available
Miscellaneous
book_gardening
Exclusive to:
“I see no farm in reading that.”
–Wilson
“Ughh, who cares?”
–Willow
“Wolfgang rather grow muscle than flowers.”
–Wolfgang
“Imagine, a rolling field of red flowers...”
–Wendy
“A USELESS TOME ON PLANT CULTIVATION”
–WX-78
“Dead plants tied together to help me aid living plants.”
–Wickerbottom
“Does it grow trees?”
–Woodie
“It's not wise to toy with forces beyond your comprehension.”
–Maxwell
“Much more than a collection of facts.”
–Wagstaff
“But can it tend the garden öf mine soul?”
–Wigfrid
“Why should we read about flowers when we can pick them?”
–Webber
“Hmm. Sounds suspiciously like work.”
–Walani
“Maybe I could start an herb garden.
Maybe Mme. Wickerbottom would be interested in starting a herb garden. ()”
–Warly
“Th'dirt's only good fer buryin' me treasure.”
–Woodlegs
“NARY A PICTURE TO BE FOUND”
–Wilba
“This one's nice”
–Wormwood
“I'm not digging this one.”
–Wheeler
“I prefer to learn from experience.”
–Winona
“Secret knowledge of vegetables.”
–Wortox
“A... appled...? (Sigh)”
–Wurt
“I already have my gardening badge.”
–Walter
“Do I even want to know how long the unabridged version was?”
–Wanda
Applied Horticulture grows crops and plants within 7.5 Tiles instantly, while Trees grow one stage every use. It does not work on unfertilized or withered crops or plants. Some plants, such as grass, do not grow in Winter.
Sleepytime Stories[edit | edit source]
Sleepytime Stories
"Soothing tales to send you off to dreamland."
Object Info
Perk
Puts nearby mobs to sleep.
-33 per use
5 uses
45 sec.
Can be Burned
Does not stack
Crafting
×2 ×2 ( only)
Tab
Tier
Miscellaneous
"book_sleep"
Exclusive to:
“Strange, it's just 500 pages of telegraph codes.”
–Wilson
“Bo-oring!”
–Willow
“Wolfgang's eyes feel heavy like dumbbells.”
–Wolfgang
“Does it impart an eternal slumber?”
–Wendy
“POWERING DOown...”
–WX-78
“Warm milk in book form.”
–Wickerbottom
“The perfect book for a full moon.”
–Woodie
“I haven't had a real sleep in... ages.”
–Maxwell
“Is this ink imbued with dark fuel?! Fascinating!”
–Wagstaff
“Where's the drama? The suspense?”
–Wigfrid
“It's... beddy-bye time...”
–Webber
“I don't need a book to fall asleep.”
–Walani
“It's tradition to nap after a good meal.”
–Warly
“It'll put ye into a cursed slumber.”
–Woodlegs
“FORSOOTH! 'TIS NOT WILBA'S BEDTIME!”
–Wilba
“(yawn)”
–Wormwood
“A sleeper hit.”
–Wheeler
“I already know how to sleep, thanks.”
–Winona
“Knowledge is power. Literally.”
–Wortox
“Want bedtime story!”
–Wurt
“My idea of a bedtime story has a lot more monsters in it...”
–Walter
“Not to critique the writing, but that nearly put me to sleep.”
–Wanda
Sleepytime Stories causes Mobs within 7.5 Tiles to fall asleep about 2 seconds after reading. The duration of sleep depends on the target and the effect is broken when it is attacked.
Sleepytime Stories can be used instead of the Pan Flute, for example for shaving Beefalos during the day and during mating season, calming Dragonfly, stopping Bee Queen's attacks and beetles, stopping Depths Worm and Hound attacks, slowing down or completely stopping most boss and giants attacks or stealing guarded items from Set Pieces.

On Tentacles
"Let's learn about our subterranean friends!"
Object Info
Perk
Create 3 Tentacles.
-50 per use(
5 uses
45 sec.
Can be Burned
Does not stack
Crafting
×2 ×1 ( only)
Tab
Tier
Miscellaneous
"book_tentacles"
Exclusive to:
“Someone'll get suckered into reading this.”
–Wilson
“Looks like kindling to me!”
–Willow
“Wolfgang wants many foes to fight!”
–Wolfgang
“Does this world need more beasts?”
–Wendy
“IT SUMMONS THE FLESH WOBBLERS”
–WX-78
“Hard to put this one down. It's gripping, frankly.”
–Wickerbottom
“They don't have tentacles in Canada.”
–Woodie
“What horrors shall be summoned from the depths?”
–Maxwell
“What prevents me from using this?”
–Wagstaff
“Knöwledge grants great pöwer!”
–Wigfrid
“Why are the pages all slimy?”
–Webber
“You know, I think I'm okay letting those things stay a mystery.”
–Walani
“I don't see any recipes in this at all.”
–Warly
“I've seen enough o'them ship sinkers to last me lifetime!”
–Woodlegs
“WILBA HATH NO USE FOR SUCH THINGS”
–Wilba
“About Scary Arms”
–Wormwood
“I'm guessing it's not a children's book.”
–Wheeler
“I'm not really a "book smarts" kind of gal.”
–Winona
“Imps don't disseminate precious knowledge so carelessly.”
–Wortox
“Remind me of home, florp.”
–Wurt
“Oooh, this reminds me of a radio play where a ship gets eaten by a giant squid!”
–Walter
“It's enough having to deal with them in person, why read about them too?”
–Wanda
On Tentacles creates up to 3 Tentacles between 3m to 8m. Less tentacles are created when there are more obstructions in the spawning zone. Burnt structures, destroyed walls, trees, a boulder or another tentacle are examples of obstructions.
Tentacles from On Tentacles generally produces more Tentacle Spots than it consumes. They are also a source of Tentacle Spikes, a great melee weapon.
The book can be used to build defenses or traps, as the Tentacles are hostile to most Mobs in the game. Tentacle pits can be built by having a ring of tree stumps and safely reading On Tentacles 15-21 times in that ring.
The End is Nigh![edit | edit source]
The End is Nigh!
"The world will end in fire and tribulation!"
Object Info
Perk
Generates Lightning.
-33 per use
5 uses
45 sec.
Can be Burned
Does not stack
Crafting
×2 ×1 ( only)
Tab
Tier
Miscellaneous
"book_brimstone"
Exclusive to:
“The beginning was dull, but got better near the end.”
–Wilson
“That's my favorite book!”
–Willow
“Tiny book scares Wolfgang!”
–Wolfgang
“The tome to end it all...”
–Wendy
“A BOOK OF DESTRUCTION. EXCELLENT”
–WX-78
“What could possibly go wrong?”
–Wickerbottom
“I know better than to mess with that, eh.”
–Woodie
“I've had enough fire and brimstone for one eternity.”
–Maxwell
“Oh, I do wish I could access its power.”
–Wagstaff
“This töme is brimming with Hel's fire!”
–Wigfrid
“We don't like how that one ends!”
–Webber
“Gnarly.”
–Walani
“I don't think that's my forte.”
–Warly
“It'll spell me doom, mark me words!”
–Woodlegs
“IT DOTH NOT SCARE WILBA, FOR WILBA CANNOT READ!”
–Wilba
“Fire!”
–Wormwood
“Feel good story of the year.”
–Wheeler
“I prefer hands-on learning.”
–Winona
“Who would leave such precious knowledge lying here?”
–Wortox
“Where Wicker-lady? Want to know what happen next!”
–Wurt
“Wendy already spoiled the ending for me...”
–Walter
“Hmph. Not if I can help it.”
–Wanda
The End is Nigh! generates 16 consecutive Lightning strikes between 0.75 and 3.75 tiles.
In the Reign of Giants (RoG) and Shipwrecked (SW) DLCs, any bolt of lightning from The End is Nigh! can strike Wickerbottom when used. The player should consider wearing lightning insulated clothing, such as the Eyebrella (RoG), Snakeskin Jacket/Hat (SW), or the Dumbrella (SW).

“Read a passage and call förth Ragnarök!”

“I'm not a good reader, but he is!”
–Webber
“I've got a bad feeling about this.”
–Walani
“There's not a single recipe to be found within.”
–Warly
“Devil magic thet summons demons from th'sky!”
–Woodlegs
“WILBA LIKETHS NOT THE LOOK OF IT”
–Wilba
“Scary sky rocks”
–Wormwood
“Who comes up with this stuff?”
–Wheeler

Lovecraft. Although Wurt can gain sanity by reading some of the books, this is likely because she reads them for personal enjoyment rather than to utilize possibly forbidden magic.

Wagstaff: Birds of the World ("I cannot access the knowledge within it. How frustrating!"), Applied Horticulture ("Much more than a collection of facts."), Sleepytime Stories ("Is this ink imbued with dark fuel?! Fascinating!"), On Tentacles ("What prevents me from using this?"), The End is Nigh! ("Oh, I do wish I could access its power.").

summaryViHtml:
Tóm tắt

Sách là vật phẩm có thể chế tạo được bởi Wickerbottom. Chúng có thể được đọc nhiều lần với cái giá là sự tỉnh táo để kích hoạt một số loại sự kiện. Chỉ Wickerbottom và Maxwell mới có thể sử dụng sách, mặc dù Maxwell chỉ có thể lấy chúng một cách hợp pháp bằng cách trao đổi với Wickerbottom khi kết thúc Chế độ Phiêu lưu.
title: Books
wikitext:
{{Navbutton/Separation}}{{Clear Mobile Infobox}} '''Books''' are items craftable by [[Wickerbottom]]. They can be read several times at the cost of [[sanity]] to trigger several type of events. Only Wickerbottom and [[Maxwell]] can use the books, although Maxwell can only obtain them legitimately by swapping with Wickerbottom at the end of [[Adventure Mode]]. Player can continue reading the books even at 0 sanity without paying any additional sanity cost. Players may use any of the standard ways to regain [[Sanity]], consign themselves to fighting shadow creatures. ==Birds of the World== {{Object Infobox |scrapbook_description =Summon up to 20 birds to land nearby. |name = Birds of the World |icon = Birds of the World.png |image = Birds of the World Dropped.png |description = 1000 species: habits, habitats, and calls. |ingredient1 = Papyrus |multiplier1 = 2 |ingredient2 = Egg |multiplier2 = 2 ({{pic24|Wickerbottom Portrait}} only) |perk = Call [[Birds]]. |durability = 3 uses |tab = Books |burnTime = [[File:Light_Sources_Filter.png|32px|link=Fuel#Fire Fuel]] 45 sec. |canBeBurned=1 |tier = 0 |stack = Does not stack |objectSanity = -50 per use |spawnCode = "book_birds"}} {{Exclusivity|Don't Starve|Reign of Giants|Shipwrecked|Hamlet|Don't Starve Together}} {{Quotes |wilson = No point studying when I can just wing it. |willow = Less reading, more burning! |wolfgang = Book is for the birds! |wendy = Will it teach me to fly away from here? |wx78 = UPLOADING AVIAN FACTOIDS TO DATABASE... |wickerbottom = The expurgated version, but it has my favorite: Megascops kennicottii. |woodie = I already know more than enough aboot those things. |maxwell = What a wasteful misuse of magic. |wigfrid = Tö rule the skies! |webber = This one has pictures! |winona = I was never much of a book learner. |warly = I had hoped it was a poultry cookbook. |wortox = Humans share their knowledge so freely. |wormwood = Pretty pictures |wurt = Read this one, Wicker-lady! |walter = I don't want to brag... but I do have a badge in birdwatching. |wanda = Time and birds sure do fly. |wagstaff = I cannot access the knowledge within it. How frustrating! |wheeler = Will it help me get back in the air? |choose = wickerbottom|walani=I think I'll just look at real birds instead.|woodlegs=This book means to bewitch the birds!|wilba=TIS NAUGHT BUT SCRIBBLERY!}} '''Birds of the World''' calls 20 to 30 [[Birds]] to land between 1.5 and 3 [[Tile]]s. It does not work during [[Hurricane Season]] in the ''[[Shipwrecked]]'' DLC. The type of spawned Birds depends on the season and turf the birds land on. ''Birds of the World'' may need to be combined with ''Sleepytime Stories'', ideally right before the night so the birds remain asleep for the duration of the night and not just duration of the book. The birds may also be frozen with [[Ice Flingomatic]]s. There are also other sleepless way of killing, such as [[Winona's Catapult|Winona's catapults]]. This book is good for farming [[Krampus]] and [[Feather]]s when a proper farm is made on the Lunar Island. {{Clear}} ==Applied Horticulture== {{Object Infobox |burnTime = [[File:Light_Sources_Filter.png|32px|link=Fuel#Fire Fuel]] 45 sec. |canBeBurned=1 |scrapbook_description = |name = Applied Horticulture |image = Applied Horticulture Dropped.png |icon = Applied Horticulture.png |description = On the care and feeding of plants. |ingredient1 = Papyrus |multiplier1 = 2 |ingredient2 = Seeds |multiplier2 = 1 |ingredient3 = Manure |multiplier3 = 1 ({{pic24|Wickerbottom Portrait}} only) |perk = Grow crops. |durability = 5 uses |tab = Books |tier = 0 |stack = Does not stack |objectSanity = -33 per use |spawnCode = book_gardening}} {{Exclusivity|Don't Starve|Reign of Giants|Shipwrecked|Hamlet}} {{Quotes |wilson = I see no farm in reading that. |willow = Ughh, who cares? |wolfgang = Wolfgang rather grow muscle than flowers. |wendy = Imagine, a rolling field of red flowers... |wx78 = A USELESS TOME ON PLANT CULTIVATION |wickerbottom = Dead plants tied together to help me aid living plants. |woodie = Does it grow trees? |maxwell = It's not wise to toy with forces beyond your comprehension. |wigfrid = But can it tend the garden öf mine soul? |webber = Why should we read about flowers when we can pick them? |winona = I prefer to learn from experience. |warly = Maybe I could start an herb garden.
Maybe Mme. Wickerbottom would be interested in starting a herb garden. ({{pic24|Don't Starve Together icon}}) |wortox = Secret knowledge of vegetables. |wormwood = This one's nice |wurt = A... appled...? (Sigh) |walter = I already have my gardening badge. |wanda = Do I even want to know how long the unabridged version was? |wagstaff = Much more than a collection of facts. |wheeler = I'm not digging this one. |choose = warly|walani=Hmm. Sounds suspiciously like work.|woodlegs=Th'dirt's only good fer buryin' me treasure.|wilba=NARY A PICTURE TO BE FOUND}} '''Applied Horticulture''' grows crops and plants within 7.5 [[Tile]]s instantly, while [[Tree]]s grow one stage every use. It does not work on unfertilized or withered crops or plants. Some plants, such as [[Grass Tuft|grass]], do not grow in [[Winter]]. {{Clear}} ==Sleepytime Stories == {{Object Infobox |burnTime = [[File:Light_Sources_Filter.png|32px|link=Fuel#Fire Fuel]] 45 sec. |canBeBurned=1 |scrapbook_description =Puts creatures nearby to sleep. |name = Sleepytime Stories |image = Sleepytime Stories Dropped.png |icon = Sleepytime Stories.png |description = Soothing tales to send you off to dreamland. |ingredient1 = Papyrus |multiplier1 = 2 |ingredient2 = Nightmare Fuel |multiplier2 = 2 ({{pic24|Wickerbottom Portrait}} only) |perk = Puts nearby mobs to sleep. |durability = 5 uses |tab = Books |tier = 4 |stack = Does not stack |objectSanity = -33 per use |spawnCode = "book_sleep"}}{{Exclusivity|Don't Starve|Reign of Giants|Shipwrecked|Hamlet|Don't Starve Together}} {{Quotes |wilson = Strange, it's just 500 pages of telegraph codes. |willow = Bo-oring! |wolfgang = Wolfgang's eyes feel heavy like dumbbells. |wendy = Does it impart an eternal slumber? |wx78 = POWERING DOown... |wickerbottom = Warm milk in book form. |woodie = The perfect book for a full moon. |maxwell = I haven't had a real sleep in... ages. |wigfrid = Where's the drama? The suspense? |webber = It's... beddy-bye time... |winona = I already know how to sleep, thanks. |warly = It's tradition to nap after a good meal. |wortox = Knowledge is power. Literally. |wormwood = (yawn) |wurt = Want bedtime story! |walter = My idea of a bedtime story has a lot more monsters in it... |wanda = Not to critique the writing, but that nearly put me to sleep. |wagstaff=Is this ink imbued with dark fuel?! Fascinating! |wheeler=A sleeper hit. |choose = wickerbottom|walani=I don't need a book to fall asleep.|woodlegs=It'll put ye into a cursed slumber.|wilba=FORSOOTH! 'TIS NOT WILBA'S BEDTIME!}} '''Sleepytime Stories''' causes [[Mobs]] within 7.5 [[Tile]]s to fall asleep about 2 seconds after reading. The duration of sleep depends on the target and the effect is broken when it is attacked. ''Sleepytime Stories'' can be used instead of the [[Pan Flute]], for example for shaving [[Beefalo]]s during the day and during [[Beefalo|mating season]], calming [[Dragonfly]], stopping [[Bee Queen]]'s attacks and beetles, stopping [[Depths Worm]] and [[Hound]] attacks, slowing down or completely stopping most boss and giants attacks or stealing guarded items from [[Set Piece]]s. {{Clear}} ==On Tentacles == {{Object Infobox |scrapbook_description =Summon some unfriendly Tentacles nearby. |name = On Tentacles |image = On Tentacles Dropped.png |icon = On Tentacles.png |description = Let's learn about our subterranean friends! |ingredient1 = Papyrus |multiplier1 = 2 |ingredient2 = Tentacle Spots |multiplier2 = 1 ({{pic24|Wickerbottom Portrait}} only) |perk = Create 3 [[Tentacle]]s. |durability = 5 uses |tab = Books |burnTime = [[File:Light_Sources_Filter.png|32px|link=Fuel#Fire Fuel]] 45 sec. |canBeBurned=1 |tier = 2 |stack = Does not stack |objectSanity = -50 per use({{pic24|Wickerbottom Portrait|Wickerbottom}} |spawnCode = "book_tentacles"}} {{Exclusivity|Don't Starve|Reign of Giants|Shipwrecked|Hamlet|Don't Starve Together}} {{Quotes |wilson = Someone'll get suckered into reading this. |willow = Looks like kindling to me! |wolfgang = Wolfgang wants many foes to fight! |wendy = Does this world need more beasts? |wx78 = IT SUMMONS THE FLESH WOBBLERS |wickerbottom = Hard to put this one down. It's gripping, frankly. |woodie = They don't have tentacles in Canada. |maxwell = What horrors shall be summoned from the depths? |wigfrid = Knöwledge grants great pöwer! |webber = Why are the pages all slimy? |winona = I'm not really a "book smarts" kind of gal. |warly = I don't see any recipes in this at all. |wortox = Imps don't disseminate precious knowledge so carelessly. |wormwood = About Scary Arms |wurt = Remind me of home, florp. |walter = Oooh, this reminds me of a radio play where a ship gets eaten by a giant squid! |wanda = It's enough having to deal with them in person, why read about them too? |wagstaff=What prevents me from using this?|wheeler=I'm guessing it's not a children's book. |choose = wheeler |walani=You know, I think I'm okay letting those things stay a mystery. |woodlegs=I've seen enough o'them ship sinkers to last me lifetime! |wilba=WILBA HATH NO USE FOR SUCH THINGS}} '''On Tentacles''' creates up to 3 [[Tentacle]]s between 3m to 8m. Less tentacles are created when there are more obstructions in the spawning zone. Burnt structures, destroyed walls, trees, a boulder or another tentacle are examples of obstructions. Tentacles from ''On Tentacles'' generally produces more [[Tentacle Spots]] than it consumes. They are also a source of [[Tentacle Spike]]s, a great melee weapon. The book can be used to build defenses or traps, as the Tentacles are hostile to most Mobs in the game. Tentacle pits can be built by having a ring of tree stumps and safely reading On Tentacles 15-21 times in that ring. {{Clear}} ==The End is Nigh!== {{Object Infobox |burnTime = [[File:Light_Sources_Filter.png|32px|link=Fuel#Fire Fuel]] 45 sec. |canBeBurned=1 |scrapbook_description =Bring down over a dozen uncontrolled lightning strikes. |name = The End is Nigh! |image = The End is Nigh! Dropped.png |icon = The End is Nigh.png |description = The world will end in fire and tribulation! |ingredient1 = Papyrus |multiplier1 = 2 |ingredient2 = Red Gem |multiplier2 = 1 ({{pic24|Wickerbottom Portrait}} only) |perk = Generates Lightning. |durability = 5 uses |tab = Books |tier = 3 |stack = Does not stack |objectSanity = -33 per use |spawnCode = "book_brimstone"}} {{Exclusivity|Don't Starve|Reign of Giants|Shipwrecked|Hamlet|Don't Starve Together}} {{Quotes |wilson = The beginning was dull, but got better near the end. |willow = That's my favorite book! |wolfgang = Tiny book scares Wolfgang! |wendy = The tome to end it all... |wx78 = A BOOK OF DESTRUCTION. EXCELLENT |wickerbottom = What could possibly go wrong? |woodie = I know better than to mess with that, eh. |maxwell = I've had enough fire and brimstone for one eternity. |wigfrid = This töme is brimming with Hel's fire! |webber = We don't like how that one ends! |winona = I prefer hands-on learning. |warly = I don't think that's my forte. |wortox = Who would leave such precious knowledge lying here? |wormwood = Fire! |wurt = Where Wicker-lady? Want to know what happen next! |walter = Wendy already spoiled the ending for me... |wanda = Hmph. Not if I can help it. |choose = walani|wagstaff=Oh, I do wish I could access its power.|wheeler=Feel good story of the year.|walani=Gnarly.|woodlegs=It'll spell me doom, mark me words!|wilba=IT DOTH NOT SCARE WILBA, FOR WILBA CANNOT READ!}} '''The End is Nigh!''' generates 16 consecutive [[Lightning]] strikes between 0.75 and 3.75 tiles. In the ''[[Reign of Giants]]'' (RoG) and ''[[Shipwrecked]]'' (SW) DLCs, any bolt of lightning from ''The End is Nigh!'' can strike Wickerbottom when used. The player should consider wearing lightning insulated clothing, such as the [[Eyebrella]] (RoG), [[Snakeskin Jacket]]/[[Snakeskin Hat|Hat]] (SW), or the [[Dumbrella]] (SW). {{Clear}} == Joy of Volcanology == {{Item Infobox |scrapbook_description = |name = Joy of Volcanology |image = Joy of Volcanology.png |description = And the sky shall rain fire! |ingredient1 = Papyrus |multiplier1 = 2 |ingredient2 = Obsidian |multiplier2 = 2 ({{pic24|Wickerbottom Portrait}} only) |burnTime = [[File:Light_Sources_Filter.png|32px|link=Fuel#Fire Fuel]] 45 sec. |canBeBurned=1 |perk = Create [[Dragoon Egg]]. |durability = 5 uses. |tab = Books |tier = 2 |stack = Does not stack |objectSanity = 0 per use |spawnCode = "book_meteor"}} {{Exclusivity|Shipwrecked}} {{Quotes |wilson = The foreword just says "Hope you like dragoons." |willow = I'll burn through this book! |wolfgang = Book make very hot rain. |wendy = Reading is a dangerous thing. |wx78 = WHERE DOES IT PLUG IN? |wickerbottom = Rainus Magmus! |woodie = That there ain't right. |maxwell = Reading strange books has always worked out for me before. |wagstaff = Includes a formula on mastering control over volcanic eruptions. |wigfrid = Read a passage and call förth Ragnarök! |webber = I'm not a good reader, but he is! |walani= I've got a bad feeling about this. |warly = There's not a single recipe to be found within. |woodlegs = Devil magic thet summons demons from th'sky! |wilba =WILBA LIKETHS NOT THE LOOK OF IT |wormwood =Scary sky rocks |wheeler =Who comes up with this stuff? |choose = wolfgang}} '''Joy of Volcanology''' creates 4 [[Dragoon Egg]]s falling at the player's location. It replaces "On Tentacles" in the ''[[Don't Starve: Shipwrecked]]'' DLC. ''Joy of Volcanology'' has the same functionality as the [[Volcano Staff]], except it is less precise. Fallen Dragoon Eggs sometimes create Lava Pools on the ground. When Lava pools burn out they leave behind [[Rocks]] , [[Ash]] and [[Charcoal]]. Lava Pools can be extinguished directly with [[Ice]], which leaves behind a single [[Obsidian]] instead. This allows Wickerbottom to easily farm Rocks, Charcoal and Obsidian. {{Clear}} =={{Pic32|Prototype}} Tips == *Wickerbottom's perk does not synergize with effect of [[The Everything Encyclopedia]]. Therefore, both her and other survivors will require at least [[Science Machine]] to prototype tier two magic items. {{Clear}} =={{pic32|Placeholder}} Trivia== *The text ''"''Ia! Ia!''"'' in the description for ''The End is Nigh''! refers to the phrase "Iä! Iä! Cthulhu fhtagn!", a chant from the [[Wikipedia:Cthulhu|Cthulhu]] Mythos of [[Wikipedia:H. P. Lovecraft|H. P. Lovecraft]]. *The sanity reduction from reading possibly could suggest that these books contain knowledge which humans were never meant to know, similar to the ''Necronomicon'' and ''Pnakotic Manuscripts'' from the writings of H.P. Lovecraft. Although Wurt can gain sanity by reading some of the books, this is likely because she reads them for personal enjoyment rather than to utilize possibly forbidden magic. *According to Wilson in ''Don't Starve Together'', ''Sleepytime Stories'' is "just 500 pages of telegraph codes". This implies that the book bores those affected to sleep. *''Sleepytime Stories'' and ''The End is Nigh!'' were originally named ''Bedtime Stories'' and ''Conflagration'' respectively.(Archive) [https://archive.ph/XH6fH Character Rebalance Discussion Thread]. Posted on June 12, 2013. *Previously, Don't Starve Together also featured the book "Applied Horticulture", but with the ''Reap What You Sow'' update, which redesigned the farming system, it was replaced by the book "Horticulture, Abridged". *When Wickerbottom inspects "Birds of the World", she mentions the Western Screech Owl. This is a reference to the mod the developers made titled ''[[The Screecher]]''. *Several characters introduced with the ''[[Hamlet]]'' DLC have unimplemented quotes for Wickerbottom's books: **[[Wagstaff]]: Birds of the World ("I cannot access the knowledge within it. How frustrating!"), Applied Horticulture ("Much more than a collection of facts."), Sleepytime Stories ("Is this ink imbued with dark fuel?! Fascinating!"), On Tentacles ("What prevents me from using this?"), The End is Nigh! ("Oh, I do wish I could access its power."). **[[Wheeler]]: Birds of the World ("Will it help me get back in the air?"), Applied Horticulture ("I'm not digging this one."), Sleepytime Stories ("A sleeper hit."), On Tentacles ("I'm guessing it's not a children's book."), The End is Nigh! ("Feel good story of the year."). ** [[Wormwood]] and [[Wilba]] do have quotes too. * Webber's comment on "Practical Rain Rituals" may be a reference to the superstition that killing a spider will make it rain. Less likely it references the Aztecs ritually sacrificing children to The Rain God, hoping for rain. Few other characters reference the use of a ritual. *All the books was [[:Category:Character-Specific|Character-Specific]] Item, but its changed in [[Don't Starve Mega Update]]. * Since Applied Horticulture was previously present in Don't Starve Together, character quotes remain in the files. Most of them do not differ in any way from the original game, except for the presence of quotes from exclusive characters and a modified Warly quote {{Quotes |wilson = I see no farm in reading that. |willow = Ughh, who cares? |wolfgang = Wolfgang rather grow muscle than flowers. |wendy = Imagine, a rolling field of red flowers... |wx78 = A USELESS TOME ON PLANT CULTIVATION |wickerbottom = Dead plants tied together to help me aid living plants. |woodie = Does it grow trees? |maxwell = It's not wise to toy with forces beyond your comprehension. |wigfrid = But can it tend the garden öf mine soul? |webber = Why should we read about flowers when we can pick them? |winona = I prefer to learn from experience. |warly = Maybe Mme. Wickerbottom would be interested in starting a herb garden. |wortox = Secret knowledge of vegetables. |wormwood = This one's nice |wurt = A... appled...? (Sigh) |walter = I already have my gardening badge. |wanda = Do I even want to know how long the unabridged version was? |choose = warly}} == {{pic32|Blueprint}} Gallery== Strange New Powers.jpg|Book as seen in the poster for the ''[[Don't Starve/Version History#July 2nd, 2013 - Strange New Powers|Strange New Powers]]'' update.
